text.title: TTS Caching, Preprocessing, and Gotchas
text.summary: Rules for caching by content hash, text preprocessing before TTS, streaming format selection, and critical agent gotchas.

rule.statement: Cache TTS output keyed on sha256(text + voice + speed + model). Never re-generate audio for identical inputs.
rule.rationale: TTS output is deterministic per input. Caching eliminates cost on pipeline replays and repeated phrases. Without it, every run bills the same audio.

rule.statement: Preprocess text before TTS: expand abbreviations, replace symbols, strip Markdown/HTML, insert explicit pauses at paragraph breaks.
rule.rationale: Symbols (*, #, <, >) and abbreviations (API, ETA, DB) are read literally. Raw Markdown output from LLMs sounds unnatural and confusing to listeners.

example.note: Passing raw LLM output with Markdown directly to TTS.
example.code: # BAD: asterisks, hashes, and angle brackets read literally text_to_speech("## Introduction\n**Key point**: use *italics* carefully")

import re
def preprocess_for_tts(text: str) -> str: text = re.sub(r'#{1,6}\s+', '', text) # strip Markdown headings text = re.sub(r'\*+([^*]+)\*+', r'\1', text) # strip bold/italic text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', text) # strip HTML text = text.replace('$', ' dollars ') text = text.replace('%', ' percent ') text = text.replace('API', 'A-P-I') return text.strip()

rule.statement: Use response_format="pcm" for streaming playback (lower decode latency); use "mp3" for storage and file delivery.
rule.rationale: PCM is raw uncompressed audio — no decode step in the playback chain. For storage, mp3 is universally compatible and smaller.

rule.statement: Cap single OpenAI TTS call at 4000 characters (not 4096) to avoid off-by-one truncation. For longer text, use LongTextTTS from tts-implementation.
rule.rationale: Some client versions truncate at exactly 4096 bytes, cutting the last few words. Using 4000 as the soft cap provides a safe margin.

rule.statement: Add a 1.2-second delay between rapid TTS calls on OpenAI tier 1 (50 RPM limit) to avoid rate limit errors in batch generation.
rule.rationale: 100 short audio clips generated without delay will hit the 50 RPM limit mid-batch, causing failures and requiring retry logic.

section.title: Agent gotchas

list.item: stream_to_file is synchronous and blocks the event loop — wrap in asyncio.to_thread() for async pipelines.
OpenAI TTS does not return audio duration — measure locally with pydub after generation.
Voice names are provider-specific and non-portable: "nova" (OpenAI) is not "en-US-Neural2-D" (Google). Parameterize by semantic label (news/assistant/narrator) not provider name.
Generated audio files written to /tmp may hit space limits in containerized environments with long audio — use explicit output paths.

section.reference: Semantic voice routing by content type
Agent task prompt for TTS subagent
